Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: TurboQuant-WASM – Google's vector quantization in the browser 47 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Show HN: A game where you build a GPU 42 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. The smallest ELF executable (2021) 29 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Herbie: Automatically improve imprecise floating point formulas 182 upvotes, 33 comments
  5. iNaturalist 496 upvotes, 118 comments
  6. Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs 54 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. Simple self-distillation improves code generation 359 upvotes, 110 comments
  8. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 933 upvotes, 319 comments
  9. Some Unusual Trees 164 upvotes, 51 comments
  10. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 373 upvotes, 143 comments
  11. Run Linux containers on Android, no root required 192 upvotes, 70 comments
  12. What life looks like on the most remote inhabited island 22 upvotes, 4 comments
  13. The Indie Internet Index – submit your favorite sites 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. 12,000 AI-generated blog posts added in a single commit 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  15. Components of a Coding Agent 49 upvotes, 15 comments
  16. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 479 upvotes, 222 comments
  17. The most-disliked people in the publishing industry 64 upvotes, 23 comments
  18. Training mRNA Language Models Across 25 Species for $165 50 upvotes, 17 comments
  19. The Cathedral, the Bazaar, and the Winchester Mystery House 104 upvotes, 43 comments
  20. When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems 22 upvotes, 6 comments
  21. Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years 234 upvotes, 144 comments
  22. Iran's Network of Cameras Bolsters Air Defenses, Expert Says 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Author of "Careless People" banned from saying anything negative about Meta 317 upvotes, 220 comments
  24. Electrical Transformer Manufacturing Is Throttling the Electrified Future 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 928 upvotes, 719 comments
  26. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 155 upvotes, 119 comments
  27. Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens 30 upvotes, 17 comments
  28. Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor 162 upvotes, 160 comments
  29. German men 18-45 need military permit to leave country for longer than 3 months 173 upvotes, 192 comments
  30. Mbodi AI (YC P25) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 5 April 2026. Source here.
